title: Wagner Moura Best Actor Oscar Prospects - The Ringer
text: Performance Assessment: The article argues that Wagner Moura delivers the performance of the year in The Secret Agent, characterizing it as superior to competing nominees despite his underdog status. Critic Adam Nayman emphasizes that Moura achieves excellence through restraint rather than excess, contrasting his approach with more bombastic turns by rivals like Timothée Chalamet and Leonardo DiCaprio. Current Odds: Moura faces an uphill battle. While he won the Golden Globe—described as a historic victory—and claimed the Cannes Best Actor prize, Michael B. Jordan and DiCaprio are considered stronger contenders heading into the Academy Awards. The piece notes that Chalamet's perceived backlash has benefited Jordan considerably. Key Strengths: Moura's campaign emphasizes political engagement over self-promotion. He discussed ICE, military dictatorship, and Brazilian democracy on Pod Save America, positioning himself as substantively engaged with his film's themes rather than pursuing typical awards-season theatrics. The performance itself demonstrates no vanity in his acting and remarkable generosity with scene partners, including child actor Enzo Nunes and nonprofessional performers. Upset Potential: An Oscar win would represent the biggest Best Actor surprise since Roberto Benigni's 1999 victory—making Moura's candidacy genuinely compelling for those favoring underdog narratives.
